message.content:
<deliberation>
- Parsed main nodes: REG-092 (Art77(1)), REG-093 (Art77(2)), REG-095 (Art78(2)).
- Located policy statements relevant to complaints/judicial remedies. Policy explicitly mentions Art 77 complaint right but not SA duties (Art77(2)) or judicial remedy timelines (Art78(2)).
- Cross-references:
  - Art77(2) expressly references Article 78; referenced_nodes provide Art78(1),(3),(4), which were considered as contextual backdrop even though the policy does not address SA procedures or judicial remedies.
  - Art78(2) references Arts 55 and 56 (competence), but these were not provided; no additional references used.
- Deviation assessment:
  - REG-092: Policy expands the grounds for a complaint from “infringes this Regulation” to “infringes data protection law” (broader than GDPR text). Classified as a Severity deviation (over-inclusive). Omitted venue details (Member State of residence/work/infringement) are treated as Non-Deviation per instructions on omissions.
  - REG-093: No statements about SA informing complainants on progress/outcome or mentioning judicial remedy; omission => Non-Deviation.
  - REG-095: No statements about judicial remedies or three-month timeline; omission => Non-Deviation.
- Evidence requirement satisfied for the single deviation (REG-092) with exact quotes from both GDPR and the policy.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "… if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.",
        "policy_quote": "In accordance with Art 77 GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes data protection law.",
        "mismatch": "The policy expands the grounds from infringements of “this Regulation” (GDPR) to any “data protection law,” which is broader than Art 77(1).",
        "analysis": "Art 77(1) limits the complaint trigger to GDPR infringements; the policy offers the right for any data protection law infringement. This is an over-inclusive commitment (not non-compliance) compared to the GDPR clause."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-093",
```
